To determine the loading dose, we need to focus on what parameter dictates the initial concentration needed to reach therapeutic levels quickly. From the evidence provided, it's clear that the volume of distribution (V_d) plays a crucial role since it quantifies how much space a drug occupies in body fluids relative to total body fluid content. A larger V_d means the drug distributes extensively throughout tissues before reaching peak plasma levels, thus requiring a greater initial dose to saturate this distributed pool effectively. Hence, the loading dose indeed relies heavily on the volume of distribution.
